methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing vertebrate fossil samples from specified localities for further analysis.

steps.description: The fossil specimens were collected by the authors and other collaborators during several field expeditions between 2007 and 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil specimens from field expeditions

steps.description: Large specimens were surface collected from the outcrops of both localities, while micro-vertebrates and microfossil remains were found only in the NCC locality.
steps.searchable_summary: surface collect large specimens and microfossils

steps.description: A total of ~ 200 kg of conglomerates and sandstones were screen-washed using standard sieves with up to 0.5 mm mesh.
The micro-specimens were sorted using a stereomicroscope.
Images were captured with a Leica MZ16F multifocal stereomicroscope and a Scanning Electronic Microscope (JEOL JSM-6010).
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of specimens with microscopes

methods.description: Procedure for identifying the taxonomic classification of fossil specimens and estimating generic richness using rarefaction and extrapolation sampling curves.

steps.description: Taxonomic identification involved an extensive bibliographic review and comparison with fossil and extant specimens housed at Argentina.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taxonomic identification through bibliographic review

steps.description: In order to estimate the generic richness and assess the completeness of the sampling, we performed a rarefaction and extrapolation sampling curve.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate generic richness using rarefaction and extrapolation

steps.description: We computed and plotted the rarefaction and extrapolation sampling curves using the iNEXT package available in R.
steps.searchable_summary: plot rarefaction and extrapolation curves using iNEXT in R
